signals.idea:
Sync Discord `_resources` channel posts into banodoco-website as auto-published resources, with a "Made with this" section containing reply media and a Discord-style comments thread.

# Context

Single shared Supabase project (`ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf`) backs both brain-of-bndc (Discord archive bot, Python, Railway) and banodoco-website (React/Vite). Discord resource posts live in 5 forum channels: `resources` (1149372684220768367), `flux_resources` (1275200992136400967), `wan_resources` (1373291419434877078), `ltx_resources` (1457981813120176138), `acestep_resources` (1472633200491626526). Each is a forum, so every message has a `thread_id`; an OP is where `message_id == thread_id`. Archive pipeline already writes every message + reaction into `discord_messages`. Current volume: ~15 qualifying OPs in the last 30 days.

# Trigger + scope

- Thread OP in any of the 5 `_resources` channels with `reaction_count >= 5` (any emoji counts) becomes an `assets` row.
- Backfill: last 60 days on first run, chronological oldest→newest, resumable via per-channel cursor.
- Ongoing: polled every 10 min.
- No un-listing if reactions later drop.
- Auto-publish `admin_status='Listed'`; admin kill switch via `assets.is_hidden`.
- Each reply in the thread becomes an `asset_comments` row. Reply attachments become `media` rows linked via `asset_comment_media`. OP attachments go via existing `asset_media`.

# Schema deltas

Extend `assets`:
- `source` TEXT ('manual' | 'discord_import')
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NULL
- `discord_channel_id` BIGINT NULL
- `imported_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `last_synced_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `reactions_reached_threshold_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_hidden` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

New `asset_comments`:
- `id` UUID PK
- `asset_id` UUID FK → assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT NOT NULL
- `author_member_id` BIGINT FK → discord_members(member_id)
- `content` TEXT
- `reply_to_comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) NULL (resolves from Discord `reference_id`)
- `reply_to_discord_message_id` BIGINT NULL (denormalized for resolvability even when the parent isn't imported)
- `reaction_count` INT DEFAULT 0
- `discord_created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `discord_edited_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_deleted` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
- `created_at`, `updated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ

New `asset_comment_media`:
- `comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `sort_order` INT DEFAULT 0
- PK (comment_id, media_id)

`media` changes:
- Add `'discord-comment'` as a valid `classification` value
- Provenance stored in `media.metadata` JSONB: `{discord_message_id, discord_channel_id, discord_attachment_id, original_cdn_url, imported_at}`
- Dedup by `discord_attachment_id` (query `metadata->>'discord_attachment_id'`)

New `media_import_jobs` queue:
- `id` UUID PK
- `discord_attachment_id` BIGINT UNIQUE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT
- `target_kind` TEXT ('asset_media' | 'asset_comment_media')
- `target_id` UUID (asset_id or comment_id depending on target_kind)
- `original_cdn_url` TEXT
- `filename` TEXT, `content_type` TEXT, `size_bytes` BIGINT
- `status` TEXT ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'failed' | 'skipped')
- `attempts` INT DEFAULT 0
- `last_error` TEXT
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) NULL (set on done)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `locked_until` TIMESTAMPTZ

Add indexes for: `assets.discord_thread_id`, `asset_comments(asset_id, discord_created_at)`, `asset_comments.discord_message_id`, `media_import_jobs(status, locked_until)`.

RLS on new tables: public read for `asset_comments` / `asset_comment_media` (matches `asset_media`); service-role only for `media_import_jobs`.

# Runtime

Two Supabase Edge Functions + pg_cron:

**`discord-resource-promoter`** (cron: every 10 min; ~30s wall clock):
1. For each of the 5 channels, advance the per-channel cursor (`discord_resource_sync_cursors` table: `channel_id`, `last_processed_created_at`, `last_backfill_cursor_at`, `backfill_complete` BOOL).
2. Discover qualifying thread OPs: `discord_messages` where `channel_id IN (...)`, `message_id = thread_id`, `reaction_count >= 5`, `created_at > cursor`.
3. Upsert `assets` rows keyed on `discord_thread_id`. Populate: `name` (first line of content, truncated), `description` (full content), `type` (infer via channel → 'lora' | 'tool'; default 'tool'), `member_id` (from author), `admin_status='Listed'`, `source='discord_import'`, `reactions_reached_threshold_at=NOW()`.
4. For each imported thread, fetch all replies: `discord_messages WHERE thread_id = X AND message_id != thread_id`. Upsert `asset_comments` keyed on `discord_message_id`. Resolve `reply_to_comment_id` from Discord `reference_id` where possible.
5. For every attachment (OP and replies), upsert a `media_import_jobs` row if not already present. Use Discord `attachment.id` as dedup key.
6. Update `assets.last_synced_at`.
7. Paginate/time-slice to stay under 30s budget; advance cursor even on partial progress.

**`discord-media-importer`** (cron: every 2 min; up to 400s):
1. Claim up to N pending jobs via `UPDATE ... SET status='in_progress', locked_until=NOW()+interval '10m' WHERE status='pending' AND (locked_until IS NULL OR locked_until < NOW()) ... RETURNING *`. Re-claim stuck `in_progress` past `locked_until`.
2. For each job:
   - Refresh Discord CDN URL (use bot token via Discord API `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}` to get current signed URL — mirror logic from existing `refresh-media-urls` edge function).
   - Stream download from Discord CDN.
   - If video/gif: stream upload to Cloudflare Stream via tus; poll until `readyToStream`; capture `playback.hls`, `thumbnail`, `duration`.
   - If image: stream upload to Supabase Storage `user-uploads/discord-imports/{discord_message_id}/{attachment_id}-{filename}`; generate thumbnail via image transform if available.
   - Other (zip/json): upload to same bucket, no thumbnail.
   - Insert `media` row: `classification='discord-comment'` (or `'post'` for OP attachments — decide convention in plan), `member_id` from message author, Cloudflare/storage URLs populated, `metadata` with Discord provenance.
   - Insert junction row (`asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` based on `target_kind`).
   - Mark job `done`, set `media_id`.
3. On failure: increment `attempts`, write `last_error`, status back to `pending` with exponential backoff via `locked_until`. Skip permanently after 5 attempts (`status='failed'`).

Secrets required on Supabase Edge Function config:
- `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_API_TOKEN`
- Existing `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` implicit

# Frontend

Update `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx`:
1. Keep existing gallery strip (primary media + `asset_media`).
2. Add **"Made with this"** section below gallery — grid of all `asset_comment_media` thumbnails for the asset, newest first, click opens lightbox anchored to the parent comment.
3. Add **Discussion** section below — flat chronological list of `asset_comments`. Each row:
   - Author chip (avatar + global_name from `discord_members`) + timestamp
   - "Replying to @name" chip with quoted excerpt if `reply_to_comment_id` set
   - Markdown-rendered `content`
   - Attached media grid (inline video/image thumbs, click → lightbox)
   - Reaction count chip
   - "View on Discord" link (build URL from channel_id + message_id)
4. New hook `useAssetComments(assetId)` — single query joining `asset_comments` + `asset_comment_media` + `media` + `discord_members`, ordered by `discord_created_at`.
5. Resource card: small "From Discord" badge (channel name) on imported assets — subtle, not a big label.

# Observability + ops

- `media_import_jobs` table is the operational dashboard (count by status, oldest pending, failures with `last_error`).
- Errors logged to `system_logs` (existing infrastructure).
- Health check query: failed jobs in last 24h, stuck `in_progress` past lock, per-channel cursor age.
- Dry-run mode: env var `DRY_RUN=true` on promoter → logs would-be inserts without writing.

# Idempotency + edge cases to handle in plan

- Discord message edits: re-sync updates `asset_comments.content`, `discord_edited_at`.
- Discord message deletions: `discord_messages.is_deleted=true` → set `asset_comments.is_deleted=true` (UI hides).
- Attachment removal on edit: don't delete `media` (preserves history); could mark `is_deleted` on junction.
- Race: promoter tries to insert comment before OP asset exists — only process replies for threads whose asset row already exists.
- Rate limits: Discord API 50 req/s; space attachment URL refreshes.
- Cloudflare Stream failures: retry; skip after N attempts.
- `discord_members` might not yet have the author when a comment arrives — promoter should upsert a thin member row if missing (or the existing archive sync covers this — verify).
- Already-imported content: must be fully idempotent — reruns should produce zero new rows.

# Out of scope (do NOT include)

- Live-update (websocket) — polling is fine.
- Moderation UI beyond the `is_hidden` flag (no approval queue, no review surface).
- Cross-posting from website back to Discord.
- User-initiated imports ("turn this Discord post into a resource" button).
- Migrating existing manually-created `assets` rows — they stay `source='manual'`.
- Changing the archive pipeline on brain-of-bndc (the existing archive covers everything the promoter needs).

# Deliverables megaplan should produce

1. Ordered implementation phases with a clear first-working-version milestone (schema + promoter dry-run) before media rehosting is wired up.
2. Migration SQL file list (timestamped, in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`).
3. Edge function directory structure under `supabase/functions/` (promoter, importer, shared helpers — there's already a `supabase/functions/_shared/` in the workspace).
4. Hook + component additions in `banodoco-website/src/` with concrete file paths.
5. pg_cron SQL snippets to register the two scheduled invocations.
6. Test/verification steps per phase (e.g. one-channel dry run, then rehost a single attachment, then full backfill).
7. Rollback plan per phase.
8. Explicit risk register (Cloudflare quota, Discord rate limits, edge function timeout, storage cost on 60-day backfill).

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord resource promoter: created_at-based cursors will miss older threads that cross the reaction threshold after the cursor has advanced.
Discord member fallback: the proposed thin upsert is incompatible with the current members schema because username is still required.
Resource typing: importing assets as `tool` conflicts with the website’s existing `lora`/`workflow` assumptions.
Resource frontend surface: the plan targets an effectively unused modal and does not cover the route-driven ResourceDetail page that users actually hit.
Edge scheduling/infrastructure: the plan introduces new cron auth and Cloudflare polling paths without reconciling existing pg_cron and Stream webhook/backfill patterns.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The user’s added scope note about markdown-rendering resource descriptions is only partially reflected in the plan. The repo does have the exact plain-text `ResourceModal` block the note calls out at `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, but the plan never adds a concrete step for that rendering change or the requested success criterion; it only mentions using the existing markdown renderer for comment content.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: One of the explicit edge cases in the brief, attachment removal on Discord edit, is still unresolved in the plan. The proposed schema for `asset_comment_media` only has `(comment_id, media_id, sort_order)` and no visibility/deleted flag, so the plan’s note that it 'could mark `is_deleted` on junction' is not actually backed by any migration or follow-up step.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The cursor strategy in the promoter is not technically correct for the stated trigger. `discord_messages.reaction_count` is a denormalized field that gets recomputed from `discord_reactions` by the `sync_message_reactors` trigger (`supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`), so an older thread can cross the 5-reaction threshold long after its `created_at`. A promoter that only scans `created_at > cursor` will permanently miss those late-promoted OPs once the cursor advances past their creation time.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The fallback member-write described in Phase 2 would violate the current schema. `members` is the renamed `discord_members` table, and its original definition still requires `username TEXT NOT NULL` (`supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:38-64`); the archive writer in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209` also always supplies a username. The plan’s 'insert only member_id, username=NULL' fallback would fail the FK-prep path instead of rescuing it.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The plan does not thread the new asset metadata through the existing data loaders that would need it for the requested UI. `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` currently select neither `source` nor `discord_channel_id` nor `is_hidden` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`), so the proposed 'From Discord' badge and hide switch cannot work until those queries and their TypeScript shapes are expanded.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The media-import plan also skips over existing Cloudflare support that already owns part of this lifecycle. The repo has `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for writing HLS/thumbnail URLs from a Stream UID and `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for retrying unfinished ingests (`supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-backfill/index.ts:35-114`), but the new plan introduces a separate importer that polls Cloudflare to readiness itself. That duplication is a supporting-infrastructure gap unless the plan explicitly says which path becomes canonical and how the existing webhook/backfill jobs are reused or retired.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 23.25. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 17.

items.criterion: All migrations in Phase 1 apply cleanly via `supabase db reset` on a scratch branch with no errors

`assets` table gains all 7 new columns (source, discord_thread_id, discord_channel_id, imported_at, last_synced_at, reactions_reached_threshold_at, is_hidden) and a unique partial index on `discord_thread_id`
`asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media`, `media_import_jobs`, and `discord_resource_sync_cursors` tables exist with the columns, constraints, and indexes described in Steps 3–4

RLS is enabled on `asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media`, `media_import_jobs`, and `discord_resource_sync_cursors`; `asset_comments`/`asset_comment_media` have public-read SELECT policies; the latter two have no policies (service-role only)
Promoter edge function with `DRY_RUN=true` logs the set of assets/comments/media-jobs it would write and performs zero DB mutations

A live run of the promoter produces `assets` rows with `source='discord_import'`, `admin_status='Listed'`, correct `type` per channel, populated `discord_thread_id`/`discord_channel_id`, and corresponding `asset_comments` rows for replies
Running the promoter twice in a row produces zero net new `assets`, `asset_comments`, or `media_import_jobs` rows (full idempotency)

Media importer processes a single seeded `media_import_jobs` row end-to-end: creates a `media` row with the correct classification + populated Cloudflare/Storage URL, creates the matching `asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` junction row, and sets job `status='done'` with `media_id`
Rehosted video URLs return a valid HLS playlist (HTTP 200 with `#EXTM3U` in body); rehosted image URLs return HTTP 200 with a supported image content-type

A failed job (simulated by pointing at a deleted Discord message) increments `attempts`, records a `last_error`, and is re-queued with a growing `locked_until`; after 5 attempts it moves to `status='failed'` and stops retrying
pg_cron has two registered schedules (`discord-resource-promoter` every 10 min, `discord-media-importer` every 2 min) both invoking the correct edge-function URLs with service-role auth

After a full backfill cycle, qualifying OPs from all 5 channels (thread_id == message_id, reaction_count >= 5, within 60-day window) are represented in `assets` with `source='discord_import'`, and no OPs below the threshold are imported
A Discord message edit after import updates `asset_comments.content` and `discord_edited_at` on the next promoter run; a Discord message deletion sets `asset_comments.is_deleted=true`
ResourceModal shows the new 'Made with this' grid and 'Discussion' thread only for assets where `source='discord_import'`; existing manual assets render unchanged

Each rendered comment shows author avatar + name from `members`, markdown-rendered content, reply chip when applicable, inline attachment thumbnails with a working lightbox, reaction count, and a 'View on Discord' link that opens the correct message
`useAssetComments` fetches the joined data in a single Supabase query and returns comments ordered by `discord_created_at` ascending, excluding deleted rows
Resource cards show a subtle 'From Discord' badge with the channel name for imported assets
`assets.is_hidden=true` suppresses the asset from the website listing and modal
Existing website tests and Supabase type generation still pass after schema additions
Promoter wall-clock stays under 30s per invocation; importer stays under 400s — observed in edge-function logs across several runs
Health-check SQL snippets return usable output (stuck jobs, 24h failures, cursor staleness) against the live tables
No Cloudflare Stream quota / storage cost surprises during 60-day backfill — monitored via Cloudflare + Supabase dashboards after first full cycle
Per-phase rollback documentation exists and accurately reflects the migrations, cron jobs, and edge functions introduced

unresolved_flags.evidence: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

unresolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The plan does not thread the new asset metadata through the existing data loaders that would need it for the requested UI. `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` currently select neither `source` nor `discord_channel_id` nor `is_hidden` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`), so the proposed 'From Discord' badge and hide switch cannot work until those queries and their TypeScript shapes are expanded.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan does not thread the new asset metadata through the existing data loaders that would need it for the requested UI. `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` currently select neither `source` nor `discord_channel_id` nor `is_hidden` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`), so the proposed 'From Discord' badge and hide switch cannot work until those queries and their TypeScript shapes are expanded.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The media-import plan also skips over existing Cloudflare support that already owns part of this lifecycle. The repo has `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for writing HLS/thumbnail URLs from a Stream UID and `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for retrying unfinished ingests (`supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-backfill/index.ts:35-114`), but the new plan introduces a separate importer that polls Cloudflare to readiness itself. That duplication is a supporting-infrastructure gap unless the plan explicitly says which path becomes canonical and how the existing webhook/backfill jobs are reused or retired.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The media-import plan also skips over existing Cloudflare support that already owns part of this lifecycle. The repo has `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for writing HLS/thumbnail URLs from a Stream UID and `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for retrying unfinished ingests (`supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-backfill/index.ts:35-114`), but the new plan introduces a separate importer that polls Cloudflare to readiness itself. That duplication is a supporting-infrastructure gap unless the plan explicitly says which path becomes canonical and how the existing webhook/backfill jobs are reused or retired.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.
